creator: kris@obsecurity.org
text:
On Tue, Nov 28, 2006 at 02:20:15PM +0000, Frank J. Laszlo wrote:
> The following reply was made to PR ports/105934; it has been noted by GNATS.
> 
> From: "Frank J. Laszlo" <laszlof@freebsd.org>
> To: pav@FreeBSD.org,  bug-followup@FreeBSD.org
> Cc:  
> Subject: Re: ports/105934: [PATCH]: Mk/bsd.port.mk: Add X_PLIST_FILES and
>  X_PLIST_DIRS
> Date: Tue, 28 Nov 2006 09:11:57 -0500
> 
>  -----BEGIN PGP SIGNED MESSAGE-----
>  Hash: SHA256
>  
>  Pav Lucistnik wrote:
>  |> As for the 1551 ports that are installed into X11BASE, I believe that a
>  |> gradual move before XOrg would be beneficial, otherwise these ports will
>  |> be BROKEN when Xorg is moved, and cause us to rush out patches to get
>  |
>  | I don't think there will be any rush, why do you believe that? The apps
>  | can live in /usr/X11R6 or any other prefix as long as they want to.
>  |
>  
>  There will be a need to rush out changes once XOrg is moved, otherwise
>  these specific files (app-defaults, fonts) will not function properly. I
>  would rather see these things stand out so they do not get overlooked.
>  By having them in X_PLIST_*, a simple grep will show which ports need to
>  be modified once XOrg is moved, and we wont be digging through
>  pkg-plist's to find them.

I don't get what problem is being addressed here.  The strategy that
is being pursued for the xorg change is some version of the following:

a) Make ports X11BASE compliant, so they will install cleanly wherever
you tell them to.  There are apparently only ~200 ports that need to
be fixed.

b) At some point in the future set X11BASE=LOCALBASE for xorg 7.x.
There will be some fiddly bits here with things like the mtree files
but the basic idea is straightforward.

c) Find some strategy for phasing out X11BASE, over the longer term.

Kris
